statistics.name: freeThrows

statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.name: turnovers
statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.name: minutes
statistics.description: The total number of minutes played.

statistics.name: assistTurnoverRatio
statistics.description: The average number of assists a player or team records per turnover

statistics.name: rebounds
statistics.description: The total number of rebounds (offensive and defensive).

statistics.name: defensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

statistics.name: offensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the offense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot.

articles.description: Chicago Sky (7-15, 1-7 Eastern Conference) at Dallas Wings (15-8, 6-6 Western Conference)

articles.headline: Seattle takes on Washington after Hiedeman's 20-point outing

articles.headline: Los Angeles Sparks vs. Chicago Sky - Game Highlights
articles.description: Watch the Game Highlights from Los Angeles Sparks vs. Chicago Sky, 07/10/2026

images.name: Los Angeles Sparks vs. Chicago Sky - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Los Angeles Sparks vs. Chicago Sky, 07/10/2026

articles.headline: Nneka Ogwumike has a double-double to lead the Sparks past the Sky, 102-87
articles.description: — Nneka Ogwumike had 25 points, 12 rebounds and five assists, Erica Wheeler added 15 points and eight assists, and the Los Angeles Sparks beat the Chicago Sky 102-87 on Friday night.

images.name: Los Angeles Sparks vs. Chicago Sky - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Los Angeles Sparks vs. Chicago Sky, 07/10/2026

articles.headline: Valkyries beat the Sun 79-64 for a franchise-record 7th straight win
articles.description: — Veronica Burton scored 17 points, Janelle Salaun had 16 points and four 3-pointers off the bench, and the Golden State Valkyries beat the Connecticut Sun 79-64 on Friday night for a franchise-record seventh straight victory.

images.name: Connecticut Sun vs. Golden State Valkyries - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Connecticut Sun vs. Golden State Valkyries, 07/10/2026

articles.headline: WNBA regular-season record crowd of 20,996 watch Wings beat Tempo
articles.description: Paige Bueckers scored 34 points and Dallas beat expansion Toronto 108-95 on Friday night in front of a WNBA regular-season record crowd of 20,996 at Bell Centre.

images.name: Bell Centre [600x400]
images.caption: A WNBA regular-season record crowd of 20,996 at Bell Centre in Montreal watched the Dallas Wings defeat the Toronto Tempo on Friday night.

images.name: Paige Bueckers drops 34 in Wings' win vs. Tempo

images.caption: The Sun are struggling, but Leila Lacan continues to put up useful numbers for fantasy.

images.caption: A'ja Wilson is the leading scorer in fantasy women's basketball this season.

articles.headline: Natasha Cloud, Chicago Sky might be perfect fit after all
articles.description: After a tumultuous offseason that left her without a team at the start of training camp, Cloud and the Sky hope the best lies ahead.

images.name: Natasha Cloud, laughing with teammate [1296x729]
images.caption: Liberty GM Jonathan Kolb said his team was fortunate to have Natasha Cloud on the roster in 2025. "She’s a phenomenal teammate," he said.

images.name: Chicago Sky [1296x729]
images.caption: Chicago Sky general manager Jeff Pagliocca was active in free agency, bringing in Natasha Cloud, Azura Stevens and Skylar Diggins, and signing undrafted free agent and Sydney Taylor, to play alongside Kamilla Cardoso.

images.name: Phoenix Mercury vs. Chicago Sky - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Phoenix Mercury vs. Chicago Sky, 07/07/2026

articles.headline: Hammon locks up spot as WNBA All-Star coach with Aces' win
articles.description: The Aces' Becky Hammon will join Minnesota's Cheryl Reeve as the two WNBA All-Star coaches in Chicago on July 25.

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Las Vegas Aces vs. Chicago Sky - Game Highlights) 2026/07/04 ESHEET

article.story: LAS VEGAS -- — NaLyssa Smith had 29 points and eight rebounds, Chelsea Gray made two key jumpers in the final minute of overtime, and the short-handed Las Vegas Aces beat the Chicago Sky 98-90 on Friday night. Las Vegas (15-5), which was without A’ja Wilson (right leg) for a second straight game, scored 10 straight points in overtime to secure the 500th win in franchise history. Las Vegas had a successful challenge with 1:08 remaining in overtime to take away a Chicago basket that would have pulled the Sky within two points. Then, Gray made a step-back jumper from the free-throw line to extend the Aces' lead to 94-88 with 48 seconds left. Gray added another jumper from near the same spot at 17.1 to make it 96-88. Gray finished with 18 points and six assists for Las Vegas. Jewell Loyd added 19 points and Jackie Young had 16 points. Smith went 11 of 14 from the field and 7 of 10 at the free-throw line Skylar Diggins led Chicago (6-14) with 19 points and Kamilla Cardoso had 16 points and 10 rebounds. Natasha Cloud added 15 points, and Courtney Vandersloot and Azura Stevens each scored 11. The Sky took their first lead of the game, 82-81, during a 12-0 run in the fourth quarter. Gray tied it at 86-all with 22.1 seconds left in regulation on another jumper in the paint. Up next Sky: At Phoenix on Tuesday night. Aces: Hosts Indiana on Sunday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
